regime_justification: USDJPY at 157.91 with BOJ signaling June hike possibility, JGB 10Y at 27-year high (2.55%), MOVE subdued (70), intervention risk elevated but MOF used 2 of 4 IMF windows—watchful stance warranted.

domino_stage.label: Pressure Building
domino_stage.justification: BOJ April Summary shows policymakers open to June hike, JGB yields spiking to 1997 levels, ¥/bbl at 16,130 (WTI ~102), USDJPY consolidating near 158—classic Stage 1 markers.

1w.label: Watchful
1w.driver: BOJ board member Masu urged rate hike 'at earliest stage possible' today; June 15-16 meeting 32 days out, JGB yields at 2.55% heighten carry fragility.

2w.label: Watchful
2w.driver: Intervention window constraints (2 of 4 used), oil at $102, US 10Y at 4.47% after hot PPI—setup fragile but no immediate catalyst inside 2-week horizon.

3w.label: Watchful
3w.driver: June BOJ meeting (Jun 15-16) falls in week 4-5; market pricing hike probability rising, OECD projects 2% terminal by end-2027—scenario-weighted elevated risk.

4w.label: Unlikely
4w.driver: If June BOJ delivers orderly 25bp hike and forward guidance is gradual, unwind pressure eases; 4-week window allows time for digest—scenario-weighted lower probability.

bottom_line: Stage 1 Pressure Building: BOJ's Masu called for hike ASAP, JGBs at 27-year high, ¥/bbl climbing, intervention ammo limited (2 of 4 IMF windows used). June 15-16 BOJ meeting is the pivot—carry fragility rising, vol still low (MOVE 70, VIX 17.9), setup coiled. Watchful (C) through week 3, eases to D by week 4 if June hike is orderly.

trigger_map.note: Board member Masu urged hike ASAP today; April Summary showed openness; market pricing ~60% for June—armed but not imminent.
2 of 4 IMF windows used (3-day clusters); USDJPY 157.91, 18 pips below 160; MOF-Bessent met May 12, no action—armed but constrained.

trigger_map.trigger: JGB 10Y break above prior cycle high
trigger_map.note: 2.55% = highest since 1997; BOJ April Summary + OECD 2% terminal forecast driving repricing—firing now.

trigger_map.trigger: Energy / Hormuz shock pushing ¥/bbl > prior peak
trigger_map.note: ¥/bbl at 16,107; WTI $102; Hormuz ~80% transit cut; IEA warns inventory drawdown; 1-2 week catalyst window if ceasefire fails.
MOVE at 70, down from 71; rates vol subdued despite hot PPI and JGB surge—complacency, not stress.

narrative.p1: USDJPY at 157.91 (+0.03%), consolidating 18 pips below the 160 watch line after suspected MOF intervention burned 2 of its 4 IMF-allowed 3-day windows. Overnight, BOJ board member Masu called for rate hike 'at earliest stage possible' if no clear economic downturn—most hawkish BOJ voice in weeks. JGB 10Y spiked to 2.55%, highest since 1997, as markets price June hike probability and OECD's 2% terminal forecast. US 10Y touched 4.49% (highest since Jul 2025) after hot PPI, then eased to 4.47%. WTI at $102 (+7.6% in 5 days) on Iran conflict and Hormuz transit collapse; IEA warns inventory drawdown at record pace. BTC broke below $80k, down $2k in 3 days on $635M ETF outflows. VIX 17.9, MOVE 70—vol still low despite macro stress. No stage change from yesterday's 1 (Pressure Building), but June BOJ meeting (15-16) now clearly the pivot event.
narrative.p2: Computed anchors: US-JP 10Y spread compressed to 192 bps (4.47 - 2.55), down 5 bps as JGB yields surge faster than UST. USDJPY 5-day pace +1.36%, well below MOF 'pace panic' threshold but drift is persistent. ¥/bbl at ¥16,107 (102 × 157.91), import-cost pressure at multi-year high and cited by BOJ April Summary as inflation risk. Carry-to-vol proxy unavailable (1M implied vol data stale). USDJPY 60d mean ~156.5, so spot at 157.91 is stretched ~90 pips above—mild overshoot, not extreme. We are squarely in Stage 1 (Pressure Building): BOJ rhetoric shifting (Masu today, April Summary showed June hike openness), JGB yields drifting up (2.55% = firing trigger), ¥/bbl rising (16,107), USDJPY pace accelerating from early May low of 155.76. Grades: 1w C, 2w C, 3w C, 4w D. Applied unwind prior as tiebreaker for 1w and 2w: data is mixed (low vol, but hawkish BOJ + JGB spike + intervention ammo depleting), so one notch more alert than a pure 'D' read. 3w and 4w scenario-weighted: if June BOJ delivers orderly hike and vol stays low, unwind pressure eases by week 4. No application of prior to 3w/4w; scenario logic dominates.
narrative.p3: BTC at $79,574, down 0.9% and below $80k for first time in 6 weeks. $635M ETF outflows May 13 led by BlackRock IBIT ($285M)—largest single-day bleed in 2 months. Open interest direction unavailable, but derivative liquidations accelerated per reports, suggesting levered longs capitulating. BTC-DXY 30d correlation data unavailable. Qualitatively, BTC decoupling from equities (Nikkei +0.84%, S&P +0.58% yesterday) and tracking rates-vol regime instead—classic risk-off rotation. Crypto liquidity draining as real yields rise (10Y at 4.47%, inflation 3.8%) and Fed hold priced through Dec. For crypto positioning: BTC breaking $80k support opens $75.8k (Apr breakout zone) as next major level. If carry unwind accelerates (Stage 3+), expect BTC to drain hard as margin calls force deleveraging across all risk assets. Current read: early risk-off, not capitulation—but the tape is turning.

horizon_rationale.w1_w2: 1w and 2w both graded C (Watchful). Masu's hawkish call today raises June hike probability, but June meeting is 32 days out—outside the 1-2w window. Immediate catalyst is low, but setup is fragile: JGB yields firing (2.55%), intervention ammo limited (2 of 4 windows used), oil at $102, and MOVE/VIX complacency. Applied unwind prior as tiebreaker; without it, data leans D (low vol, no imminent BOJ action), but carry fragility and policy repricing justify C.
horizon_rationale.w2_w3: 2w C → 3w C. June BOJ meeting (Jun 15-16) falls in week 4-5 (32 days out), so week 3 is the lead-in. Market will start positioning for June decision, especially if Masu's comments are echoed by other board members or if May CPI (due late May) runs hot. Intervention risk also elevated in week 3 if USDJPY tests 160 again and MOF has only 2 windows left. 3w remains C (Watchful) because the catalyst is visible and the setup is coiled, but not yet imminent.
horizon_rationale.w3_w4: 3w C → 4w D. Week 4 (28 days out) sits just before June BOJ meeting. If the meeting delivers an orderly 25bp hike with gradual forward guidance, unwind pressure eases—markets can digest a telegraphed move. 4w grade D (Unlikely) is scenario-weighted: base case is gradual normalization, not shock. If instead BOJ holds or delivers uber-hawkish surprise, 4w would flip back to C or B—but median scenario is orderly hike, hence D.

watch_list: BOJ board member speeches May 15-20 (Masu was first; watch for Tamura, Nakagawa echoing)
USDJPY test of 160.00 and MOF rate-check rumors (only 2 of 4 IMF intervention windows remaining)
WTI crude and Iran ceasefire headlines (Hormuz transit at 20% of normal, IEA inventory warning)
May Tokyo CPI (due late May) and any upside surprise that raises June BOJ hike probability
BTC $75,800 support test (April breakout zone; break signals deeper crypto liquidity drain)
